senses.definition: the act or process of producing offspring or offspring in a lineage
examples: scientists studied progeneration patterns in the species
advances in progeneration research aim to extend healthy family lines

senses.definition: relating to or capable of promoting generation or reproduction
examples: the progenerative traits were selected for in the breeding program
progenerative health strategies focus on reproduction outcomes

senses.definition: plural of progeny; the offspring or descendants of a person, animal, or plant
examples: the king celebrated the birth of his progenies
these bushes yield plentiful progenies each season

senses.definition: relating to the generation or creation of offspring
examples: progenital lineage tracing revealed ancestral connections
research into progenital traits continued for generations

senses.definition: producing or generating; relating to generation
examples: a progenitive function in the organism
progenitive forces shaped the ecosystem

senses.definition: the quality of being progenitive; the ability to generate offspring
examples: the progenitiveness of the species varied across environments
breeders evaluated progenitiveness before mating pairs

senses.definition: a person who creates or first introduces something; an ancestor from whom others descend
examples: the scientist was regarded as a progenitor of the modern theory
the progenitors of the city settled this valley centuries ago

senses.definition: relating to a progenitor or the origin of a lineage
examples: progenitorial records were kept by the clan
the manuscript describes progenitorial lineages

senses.definition: plural of progenitor; multiple ancestors or originators
examples: the town's progenitors were explorers
these seeds carry the genomes of their progenitors

senses.definition: the state or role of being a progenitor; the position of originator
examples: the progenitorship of the reform movement was widely discussed
that family holds a famous progenitorship in local lore

senses.definition: plural of progenitorship
examples: several progenitorships were established in the colony
progenitorships in the lineage were traced meticulously

senses.definition: a female progenitor; a female ancestor or originator
examples: the elder progenitress of the clan shared stories
she was revered as the progenitress of many generations

senses.definition: plural of progenitress; female progenitors
examples: the progenitresses of the village passed down how to harvest
a line of progenitresses governed the estate

senses.definition: plural feminine form of progenitor in some languages; female originators
examples: the legends credit various progenitrices with shaping the realm
her role as one of the progenitrices is celebrated in folklore

senses.definition: a female progenitor or originator; a mother or female ancestor
examples: she is revered as the progenitrix of the family traditions
the myth speaks of the progenitrix who began the lineage

senses.definition: plural of progenitrix; female progenitors
examples: the tales mention several progenitrixes who founded towns
her ancestors, progenitrixes of note, are honored

senses.definition: alt spelling for progenitrixes; female progenitors
examples: the progenitrixes are remembered in family lore
scholars compare the progenitrixes across myths

senses.definition: plural feminine form of progenitrix; female progenitors
examples: various progenitrices shaped the early culture
the lineage credits several progenitrices with the ascent

senses.definition: alternative spelling for progenitrix; a female progenitor
examples: she was celebrated as the progenitrix of the clan
the legend centers on the progenitrix who began the line

senses.definition: plural spelling variant of progenitrix; female progenitors
examples: the progenitrixes appear in regional folklore
research compares the progenitrixes across communities

senses.definition: the act or system of inheritance and succession; lineage by birth
examples: the title passed by progeniture rather than choice
debates about hereditary succession in progeniture terms

senses.definition: plural of progeniture; systems of heredity or lineages
examples: different cultures have distinct progenitures
scholars study the various progenitures of royal families

senses.definition: the state or quality of being a progeny; offspring; descendants
examples: the progenity of the species is well documented
research into fetal progenity sheds light on development

senses.definition: one's children or offspring; the young of an animal or plant
examples: she has two progeny
the tree produced healthy progeny this year

senses.definition: tending to grow or orient toward light or gravity in a way typical of root or shoot responses
examples: the plant exhibited progeotropic growth toward the light
progeotropic responses are studied in botany

senses.definition: the growth or bending of parts of organisms in response to gravity or light, typically downward or toward gravity in roots
examples: progeotropism guides root growth in many species
the study of progeotropism reveals how plants orient themselves

senses.definition: a rare genetic condition that causes children to age rapidly
examples: progeria is characterized by accelerated aging in young children
research into progeria aims to slow aging processes

senses.definition: plural of progeria; multiple cases of the aging-rare genetic condition
examples: several progerias were reported in the study
advances help treat various progerias

senses.definition: early stage of germination before visible growth; initial development
examples: progermination occurs soon after seeds imbibe water
conditions were right for progermination to begin
